categories.website: Specific to GIDF — its rituals, history, the people who curate and teach it
categories.easy: Fun cultural anchors — Bollywood, recognisable Indian dance moves and songs
categories.hard: Deep cuts — classical history, intricate styles, expert Bollywood trivia

tags.gidf: Festival rituals, history, manifesto, organisers, gala — and Shoonya as a name
tags.swapnil: The curator: training, lineage, contribution, what he teaches at GIDF
tags.faculty: Other GIDF teachers — achievements, collaborations, lineage
tags.madhuri: Madhuri Dixit specifically — supports the gala medley callback

questions.q: How many editions of GIDF have there been to date, including 2026?
questions.fact: GIDF began in 2023 — this is the 4th edition. "Three days. Seven master teachers. One community that keeps coming back."

questions.q: GIDF began in which year?
questions.fact: The first Gent India Dans Festival ran 5–7 May 2023 at Shoonya. We've been every year since.

questions.q: Which GIDF 2026 faculty member has taught at every single edition since 2023?
questions.fact: Vaishali Sagar is GIDF's anchor — she's been here all four years. The community grows, Vaishali stays.

questions.q: GIDF's morning ritual is called Rise & Riyaaz. What does *Riyaaz* mean in Hindi?
questions.fact: Riyaaz is the daily, disciplined practice every classical Indian musician and dancer keeps. We start each GIDF morning with it — together.

questions.q: The break ritual *Spill the Chai* is named for the tea — but what's its second, unspoken function?
questions.fact: The bar area at Shoonya turns into a 20-minute community circle between sessions. The chai is the excuse — the conversation is the point.

questions.q: GIDF closes Sunday evening with *One Last Gupshup*. What does *Gupshup* mean?
questions.fact: Gupshup is a casual, warm conversation — exactly how GIDF ends every year. Not with a bow, but with a chat.

questions.q: Complete the GIDF tagline: 'Three days. Seven master teachers. One ____'
questions.fact: It's the community that returns — students, teachers, alumni — that makes GIDF a festival and not just a workshop weekend.

questions.q: GIDF opens its manifesto with: 'Indian dance is ____.' What's the missing word?
questions.fact: "Indian dance is not one thing." Kalbeliya, Chhau, Lavani, Kuthu, Giddha — each its own history, its own community. That plurality is the festival.

questions.q: Whose medley is the centrepiece performance — Act #17 — at the GIDF 2026 Gala Showcase?
questions.fact: GIDF students are dancing a Madhuri Dixit medley as the gala finale. Choli to Choli to Maar Daala. The Dhak Dhak Girl earned her tribute.

questions.q: GIDF is curated by Swapnil Dagliya. Which two organisations co-host it?
options: ABC a Bollywood Company + Shoonya Dance Centre
questions.fact: ABC a Bollywood Company brings the production muscle. Shoonya Dance Centre brings the home. Swapnil curates.

questions.q: Shampa Gopikrishna is teaching at GIDF 2026. What's notable about her presence this year?
questions.fact: Shampa was at GIDF 2025, and she's back for 2026 — her second year in a row. (She was scheduled for 2024 too, but her visa came in too late.)

questions.q: Shoonya — the dance centre that hosts GIDF every year — gets its name from a Sanskrit word meaning…?
questions.fact: Shoonya means "zero" or "emptiness" — the open, unmarked space from which all movement emerges. Co-founded by Swapnil Dagliya.

questions.q: Where did GIDF curator Swapnil Dagliya train abroad in dance?
options: Broadway Dance Center NYC + Opus Ballet Florence
Pina Bausch Wuppertal + LINES Ballet SF
questions.fact: Swapnil trained at Broadway Dance Center in New York and Opus Ballet in Florence — blending Indian folk roots with ballet, jazz, and contemporary.

questions.q: Swapnil Dagliya was born near which Indian city?
questions.fact: Swapnil grew up near Mumbai before training internationally and bringing Indian dance to Belgium.

questions.q: Swapnil co-founded which Ghent dance institution that now hosts GIDF?
questions.fact: Shoonya — Sanskrit for "zero" — is Swapnil's dance home in Ghent. The festival lives there because the festival started there.

questions.q: How long has Swapnil Dagliya been bringing Indian dance to Belgium?
questions.fact: Over ten years. Long before GIDF began, the work was already happening week-in, week-out at Shoonya.

questions.q: Which two folk-rooted dance forms does Swapnil teach at GIDF 2026?
questions.fact: Lavani from Maharashtra and Kalbeliya from Rajasthan — two of India's most evocative folk traditions. Both come from communities, not from courts.

questions.q: Who's behind the decks as DJ at the GIDF Opening Party?
questions.fact: DJ @swapkebolly — that's Swapnil himself. The curator pulls double duty Friday night.

questions.q: Which GIDF morning ritual does Swapnil personally lead each day?
questions.fact: Swapnil opens every GIDF morning with Rise & Riyaaz — a shared, gentle warm-up to set the day's tone.

questions.q: Swapnil's dance training blends which influences?
questions.fact: He's deliberately cross-trained — Indian folk roots, Western ballet, jazz, contemporary. That mix shapes how GIDF programmes its workshops.

questions.q: GIDF faculty member Hameed Khan Kawa performed on which Indian music TV show alongside Sunidhi Chauhan?
questions.fact: Hameed has played Coke Studio Bharat with Sunidhi Chauhan, performed at Edgbaston Stadium, and collaborated with Ustad Zakir Hussain and the Gypsy Kings.

questions.q: Hameed Khan Kawa is the visionary behind which traveling Rajasthani musical ensemble?
options: Musafir, Jaipur Kawa Brass Band, Kawa Circus
questions.fact: Three groups, all his. Hameed has carried Rajasthan's tabla soul to stages around the world.

questions.q: Colleena Shakti — GIDF's Abhinaya teacher — founded a dance school in which Rajasthan town?
questions.fact: Shakti School of Dance in Pushkar. Colleena's methodology blends Natya Shastra, Yoga, and Martial Arts — over two decades in the making.

questions.q: Whose daughter is GIDF faculty member Shampa Gopikrishna?
questions.fact: Shampa is the daughter of legendary Kathak exponent Natraj Shri Gopikrishna. She started dancing at age 4.

questions.q: Shampa Gopikrishna has won India's celebrity dance reality show *Jhalak Dikhhla Jaa* how many times?
questions.fact: Two-time champion. Beyond the show, Shampa has choreographed for Monsoon Wedding, Disney's Aladdin, Padmaavat, and Ram Leela.

questions.q: Which Sanjay Leela Bhansali Bollywood films has GIDF faculty member Shampa Gopikrishna choreographed for?
questions.fact: Padmaavat and Ram Leela. Shampa's also choreographed for Monsoon Wedding and Disney's Aladdin — a Bollywood-stage CV that runs deep.

questions.q: Manasi Khanna leads which Europe-based bhangra collective?
questions.fact: Amsterdam Bhangra. Manasi has been the lead force bringing North Indian folk energy to European stages.

questions.q: Manasi Khanna performed in the dance crew for which Punjabi superstar's *Dil-Luminati* tour?
questions.fact: Diljit Dosanjh's Dil-Luminati tour — Manasi was on the dance crew for the Netherlands stop. The festival has working professionals on every billing.

questions.q: Vaishali Sagar's Mumbai dance academy *Kalanjay* has produced approximately how many alumni?
questions.fact: 5,000+ alumni. Vaishali holds a master's in Indian Folk Dance and has represented India at festivals across Italy, Turkey, South Korea, and Belgium.

questions.q: GIDF's *Desihop* collective formed in which European city in 2023?
questions.fact: Frankfurt, 2023. Three artists — Gokul Gopi, Saumya Singh, and Rayeez Mohammed — fusing Bollywood, hip-hop, Afro, and Tamil Kuthu.

questions.q: Which traditional Tamil street-dance form is GIDF's *Desihop* collective best known for?
questions.fact: Kuthu — Tamil cinema's wildest groove. Desihop's brought it to 15+ workshops across Germany.

questions.q: *Cheraw* — taught at GIDF by Vaishali Sagar — uses what as its central prop?
questions.fact: Cheraw is Mizoram's bamboo dance — long poles clapped rhythmically, dancers stepping between them. Vaishali's *Bamboo Beats* workshop is the GIDF Sunday closer.

questions.q: What is the 'A' in *ABC a Bollywood Company* — the GIDF co-organiser?
questions.fact: Just A. Brilliantly literal naming — A Bollywood Company. Founded by Swapnil.

questions.q: GIDF gala showcases happen on which night of the festival?
questions.fact: Saturday night — 20:30, Shoonya Theatre. ~90 minutes, no interval. 18 acts in one continuous flow.

questions.q: Madhuri's tribute medley in the GIDF gala is performed by whom?
questions.fact: GIDF students perform the Madhuri medley — and that's the point. The festival is built on community taking the stage.

questions.q: Which Bollywood icon is famously known as the *Dhak Dhak Girl*?
questions.fact: From the song *Dhak Dhak Karne Laga* in *Beta* (1992) — the name has stuck for over thirty years.

questions.q: AR Rahman won an Academy Award for music in which film?
questions.fact: Two Oscars in 2009 for Slumdog Millionaire — Best Original Score and Best Original Song ("Jai Ho").

questions.q: The bell anklets worn by classical Indian dancers are called…?
questions.fact: Ghungroos — a single Kathak ankle can carry 100+ bells. Each strike is a chosen sound.

questions.q: Which classical dance form is famous for its fast spins called *chakkars*?
questions.fact: Kathak — descended from the kathakas, the temple storytellers. The chakkar is its signature move.

questions.q: Bharatanatyam — one of India's oldest classical dance forms — comes from which state?
questions.fact: Tamil Nadu. Originally a temple dance, codified into proscenium form in the 20th century.

questions.q: Bhangra — the high-energy harvest dance that went global — comes from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Punjab. Started as a harvest dance, scaled to weddings, then to clubs and stadiums via the diaspora.

questions.q: Which Indian festival is celebrated by throwing colored powders to mark the arrival of spring?
questions.fact: Holi — colour, music, water, and a near-zero dress code (the white kurta will not survive).

questions.q: In *Hum Aapke Hain Koun..!* (1994), Madhuri Dixit's leading man was…?
questions.fact: Salman Khan, directed by Sooraj Barjatya — one of the highest-grossing Hindi films of the 1990s and a wedding-soundtrack institution.

questions.q: What does the word *Bollywood* literally combine?
questions.fact: Bombay (now Mumbai) + Hollywood. Coined in the 1970s — and Mumbai still calls itself Bollywood, even after the name change.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity is celebrated as *Nataraja* — the Lord of Dance?
questions.fact: Shiva as Nataraja performs the cosmic Tandava — the dance of creation and destruction. The image is iconic across Indian classical dance.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit trained extensively in which classical dance form, under Pt. Birju Maharaj?
questions.fact: Kathak. You see the training most clearly in *Maar Daala* from Devdas — pure Lucknow gharana.

questions.q: In which 1993 Madhuri Dixit film did the chart-topping song *Choli Ke Peechhe* appear?
questions.fact: Khal Nayak. The song was a national debate, a national hit, and a national anthem at every wedding for the next decade.

questions.q: Lavani — taught at GIDF by Swapnil — is the powerful semi-classical folk dance of which Indian state?
questions.fact: Maharashtra. Built around the high-pitched Dholki drum and traditionally performed by women — fierce, witty, percussive.

questions.q: Madhuri's iconic 1988 song *Ek Do Teen* was from which film?
questions.fact: Tezaab — choreographed by the legendary Saroj Khan. The song made Madhuri an overnight national star.

questions.q: Garba — the swirling Navratri dance — is the signature folk form of which Indian state?
questions.fact: Gujarat. Danced in concentric circles around a lamp or image of the Goddess — nine nights, every Navratri.

questions.q: What's the Sanskrit term for the symbolic hand gestures used throughout Indian classical dance?
questions.fact: Mudras (also called Hastas) are the dance vocabulary — 28 single-hand and 24 combined-hand gestures, each with multiple meanings depending on context. A flame, a deer, a lotus, a question. Whole stories told with the hands.

questions.q: Which classical dance form is most famous for elaborate face make-up that takes 3–4 hours to apply?
questions.fact: Kathakali — Kerala's storytelling dance-theatre. The chutti makeup transforms the dancer into divine and demonic characters.

questions.q: Pt. Ravi Shankar made which Indian instrument internationally famous in the 1960s?
questions.fact: Sitar. He taught George Harrison of The Beatles, and a wave of Indian classical influence rolled through 60s pop.

questions.q: Which Bollywood superstar is known as *King Khan* / King of Bollywood?
questions.fact: Shah Rukh Khan — also called Badshah of Bollywood. Global superstar across four decades.

questions.q: What does the name *Madhuri* literally mean in Sanskrit/Hindi?
questions.fact: Sweetness. The Dhak Dhak Girl was always going to be a household name.

questions.q: Saroj Khan — choreographer of *Ek Do Teen* and dozens of Madhuri's iconic songs — was best known as a…?
questions.fact: India's most celebrated dance director — three National Awards. She choreographed for over 2,000 songs across her career.

questions.q: *Mohiniyattam*, the elegant solo dance form from Kerala, literally means…?
questions.fact: *Dance of the enchantress* — Mohini, Vishnu's female avatar. Performed in Kerala's signature white-and-gold sari.

questions.q: Which Kerala dance form is the rare ancient classical tradition originally codified as male-only?
questions.fact: Kathakali. Men played even the female roles, in transformative makeup. Women dance Kathakali today, but the historical tradition is male-coded.

questions.q: *Diwali*, India's biggest festival, is celebrated as the festival of…?
questions.fact: Lights. Lamps, sweets, fireworks, and family — the homecoming festival.

questions.q: Devdas (2002) — the Madhuri-Aishwarya-SRK epic — was directed by which filmmaker?
questions.fact: Sanjay Leela Bhansali. The film also marked Madhuri's collaboration with her classical guru Pt. Birju Maharaj on *Maar Daala*.

questions.q: Manipuri classical dance — gentle, devotional, with elaborate skirted costumes — comes from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Manipur. Most associated with Krishna and the gopis — Raas Leela.

questions.q: Odissi — known for its sculpturesque postures — comes from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Odisha. Famous for the *tribhanga* — the three-bend posture you see in temple sculptures.

questions.q: How many forms are officially recognised as classical Indian dance by the Sangeet Natak Akademi?
questions.fact: Eight: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam, Odissi, and Sattriya — the most recent addition (recognised in 2000).

questions.q: Kuchipudi — known for its dramatic dance-drama style — is the classical dance of which state?
questions.fact: Andhra Pradesh — named after the village of Kuchipudi, where the form was preserved by Brahmin men in dance-drama performances.

questions.q: The Bollywood term for a stand-alone, often glamorous song-and-dance number not tied to plot is…?
questions.fact: *Item number* — designed as a self-contained showpiece. Often features a guest star — though Madhuri herself made *Tabaah Ho Gaye* in Kalank a leading-actress moment.

questions.q: Which is the only Chhau style traditionally performed without masks?
questions.fact: Mayurbhanj Chhau (Odisha). Seraikella (Jharkhand) and Purulia (West Bengal) both wear elaborate masks — Mayurbhanj relies entirely on facial expression.

questions.q: Pt. Birju Maharaj — Madhuri Dixit's classical guru — belonged to which Kathak gharana?
questions.fact: The Lucknow (Kalka-Bindadin) Gharana — known for grace, expressiveness (bhav), and storytelling. He choreographed *Kaahe Chhed Mohe* for Madhuri in Devdas.

questions.q: Lavani is traditionally divided into which two main types?
questions.fact: Phadachi Lavani is performed for live theatre crowds; Baithakichi Lavani is the seated, classical version. Both anchored by the Dholki drum.

questions.q: In which year was Kalbeliya inscribed on the UNESCO Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity?
questions.fact: 2010. Kalbeliya is performed by the Sapera (snake-charmer) community of Rajasthan — and the dance imitates serpentine movement.

questions.q: Who founded the Kalakshetra dance institute in 1936, leading the 20th-century revival of Bharatanatyam?
questions.fact: Rukmini Devi founded Kalakshetra in 1936 in Madras (now Chennai). She reframed Bharatanatyam from temple practice to proscenium art form.

questions.q: Bharata Muni is the legendary author of which foundational Indian performing arts treatise?
questions.fact: The Natya Shastra (~200 BCE–200 CE) — the encyclopedic source-text for Indian classical dance, drama, and music.

questions.q: How many single-hand mudras (asamyukta hastas) are described in classical Indian dance theory?
questions.fact: 28 asamyukta hastas (single-hand) and 24 samyukta hastas (combined). A vocabulary of dozens of named gestures, each with multiple meanings.

questions.q: The Bharatanatyam concept of *Tandava* represents which energy?
questions.fact: Tandava is masculine and cosmic — Shiva. Lasya is feminine and graceful — Parvati. The two are inseparable in classical theory.

questions.q: Cheraw — the bamboo dance — is the traditional folk form of which Indian state?
questions.fact: Mizoram. Long bamboo poles are clapped rhythmically while dancers step between them — originally a tribal harvest dance.

questions.q: India's Sangeet Natak Akademi — the body that officially recognises classical dance forms — was founded in which year?
questions.fact: 1953. Just six years after Independence — culture as state-building.

questions.q: In *Devdas* (2002), Madhuri Dixit's iconic dance solo *Maar Daala* draws on which classical style?
questions.fact: Kathak vocabulary throughout — though *Maar Daala* itself was choreographed by Saroj Khan. Madhuri's other Devdas piece, *Kaahe Chhed Mohe*, came from her Kathak guru Pt. Birju Maharaj.

questions.q: *Dola Re Dola* in *Devdas* (2002) featured Madhuri Dixit alongside which co-star?
questions.fact: Madhuri and Aishwarya Rai performed the duet — directed by Sanjay Leela Bhansali. The blueprint for Bollywood's two-actress dance-off ever since.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit was awarded the Padma Shri by the Government of India in which year?
questions.fact: Padma Shri in 2008 — India's fourth-highest civilian honour.

questions.q: Which 2007 film marked Madhuri Dixit's comeback to lead Bollywood roles after a five-year break?
questions.fact: Aaja Nachle (2007) — Madhuri returns as a NY-based dancer reclaiming a hometown amphitheatre. The film's title song became its own moment.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit's 2019 song *Tabaah Ho Gaye* — a Mujra-inspired classical solo — appeared in which Karan Johar-produced period romance?
questions.fact: Kalank (2019), directed by Abhishek Varman and produced by Karan Johar / Dharma Productions. Madhuri returned to a big-screen lead role in this sweeping period romance.

questions.q: Which is the principal percussion instrument of Lavani performance?
questions.fact: Lavani is built around the high-pitched Dholki — a small two-headed barrel drum. The drum sets the tempo, the dancer answers.

questions.q: Yakshagana — a centuries-old folk theatre and dance form — comes from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Yakshagana is a coastal Karnataka tradition combining dance, music, dialogue, and elaborate make-up — sometimes performed all night long.

questions.q: Pt. Ravi Shankar (sitar) famously taught and influenced which member of The Beatles?
questions.fact: George Harrison. The sitar in *Norwegian Wood* (1965) sparked a wave of Indian-classical influence in 1960s pop music.

questions.q: The signature *three-bend* sculptural posture of Odissi is called…?
questions.fact: Tribhanga — bends at the neck, waist, and knee. It gives Odissi its temple-sculpture silhouette.

questions.q: Sattriya classical dance was historically practiced in which kind of institution?
questions.fact: Sattriya was preserved by male monks (bhakatas) in the Sattras of Assam for ~500 years before being recognised as classical in 2000.

questions.q: The Kalbeliya dance is associated with which traditional community of Rajasthan?
questions.fact: The Sapera/Kalbeliya were snake handlers; their dance imitates serpentine movements. The community kept the form alive even as snake-handling itself faded.

questions.q: Mayurbhanj Chhau, Seraikella Chhau, and Purulia Chhau all arose along which broad cultural region of India?
questions.fact: All three Chhau styles emerged along the eastern tribal belt. They differ on mask use, technique, and lineage — but share martial roots.

questions.q: The dance term *Adavu* specifically refers to…?
options: A basic step / movement unit in Bharatanatyam
questions.fact: Adavus are the building blocks of Bharatanatyam — like the alphabet of the dance. Drilled for years before any choreography.

questions.q: Which film ushered in Madhuri Dixit's blockbuster era — making her a top-tier star — alongside the song *Ek Do Teen*?
questions.fact: Tezaab (1988), directed by N. Chandra. Saroj Khan's choreography turned the song into a national craze and Madhuri into a star overnight.

questions.q: Rukmini Devi Arundale's revival of Bharatanatyam was partly a response to the 20th-century stigma against which traditional community of dancers?
questions.fact: Devadasis — temple dancers — were stigmatised under colonial laws. Rukmini Devi reframed and presented the form on the proscenium stage as a respectable art.

questions.q: Which legendary female Kathak dancer was famously called *Nritya Samragni* — the Empress of Dance — by Rabindranath Tagore?
questions.fact: Sitara Devi (1920–2014). Tagore is said to have given her the title after watching her perform at age 16. A dancer's dancer.

questions.q: *Ghoomar* — the slow swirling folk dance often associated with the Padmavati legend — is most strongly tied to which Indian state?
questions.fact: Rajasthan. The signature dance of Bhil and Rajput women — performed in flowing ghagras, the swirl is the spectacle.

questions.q: Pt. Birju Maharaj choreographed Madhuri's classical Kathak solo *Kaahe Chhed Mohe* in which 2002 Bollywood film?
questions.fact: Devdas. Birju Maharaj working directly with his student Madhuri Dixit — pure Lucknow-gharana Kathak in a Bollywood frame.

questions.q: *Giddha* — Punjab's women's folk dance — is traditionally performed without which element that defines Bhangra?
questions.fact: Giddha is voice-and-clap driven — *bolian* (couplets) and handclaps — without the Dhol that anchors Bhangra. A different rhythm entirely.

questions.q: Helen — Bollywood's defining cabaret-style dancer-actress, known by a single name — appeared in approximately how many films across her full career?
questions.fact: Helen Ann Richardson Khan starred in around 700 films, defining the Bollywood *item* tradition decades before the term existed. She'd already passed 500 films by 1972.

questions.q: How many Filmfare Best Actress awards has Madhuri Dixit won?
questions.fact: Four wins: Dil (1991), Beta (1993), Hum Aapke Hain Koun..! (1995), and Dil To Pagal Hai (1998). For Devdas (2003) she won Filmfare Best Supporting Actress — a different category.
